mnemonics: Distributed Mutual Exclusion: Diners at a Restaurant
keyPoints: 15.1: Distributed Mutual Exclusion - A problem where two processes cannot use a shared resource simultaneously.
15.2: Dining Philosophers Problem - A classic example of Distributed Mutual Exclusion, where philosophers sit around a table with chopsticks.
15.3: Livelock - A situation where two processes take turns accessing a shared resource, but neither can make progress.
15.4: Deadlock - A situation where two or more processes are blocked indefinitely, each waiting for the other to release a resource.
15.5: Solution to Distributed Mutual Exclusion - Using semaphores or monitors to control access to shared resources.
